name: S8 / sigma_8 tension

observable.description: Amplitude of matter clustering on 8 Mpc/h scales, normalized by matter density. Probes how much late-time structure has grown, and is therefore sensitive to physics that suppresses or enhances gravitational clustering between recombination and today.

characteristic_scale.note: 8 Mpc/h ~ 2.5e23 m. This tension probes the late-time growth of structure on tens-of-Mpc scales.

references: Planck 2018 Results VI, A&A 641, A6 (2020), arXiv:1807.06209

measurements.reference: Heymans et al., A&A 646, A140 (2021), arXiv:2007.15632

measurements.note: CMB lensing prefers the high (Planck-like) value; the tension is primarily between weak gravitational lensing of galaxies and CMB-anchored predictions, not between all probes.
Joint analysis with harmonized scale cuts and modeling.

tension_history.note: Heymans et al. 2021 cosmic shear + galaxy clustering.

tension_history.comparison: DES Y3 + KiDS joint vs Planck 2018
tension_history.note: Persistent low-amplitude clustering on weak-lensing scales.

summary: Weak gravitational lensing surveys (KiDS, DES, HSC) consistently measure less late-time structure clustering than LambdaCDM predicts when fit to Planck CMB data. The tension sits at ~2-3 sigma and has been stable across multiple independent surveys. Galaxy clustering, cluster counts, and full-shape galaxy power spectra give a mixed picture; CMB lensing (ACT, SPT) is consistent with Planck. Whether this is a genuine signal of suppressed structure growth (e.g., a non-cold dark matter component, or modified gravity) or a baryonic-feedback / photometric systematic remains unresolved.
systematics_notes: Modeling of baryonic feedback at small scales, photometric redshift calibration, and intrinsic alignments of galaxies are the leading systematics for cosmic shear. Different scale-cut choices can move S8 by ~1 sigma. The fact that CMB lensing (a different physical probe at higher redshift) gives a Planck-like S8 favors a low-redshift / small-scale source of any new physics, if any.

references.citation: Heymans et al. (KiDS-1000), 'KiDS-1000 Cosmology: Multi-probe weak gravitational lensing and spectroscopic galaxy clustering constraints', A&A 646, A140 (2021)
Abbott et al. (DES), 'Dark Energy Survey Year 3 results: Cosmological constraints from galaxy clustering and weak lensing', PRD 105, 023520 (2022)
Madhavacheril et al. (ACT), 'The Atacama Cosmology Telescope: DR6 Gravitational Lensing Map and Cosmological Parameters', ApJ 962, 113 (2024)
Di Valentino et al., 'Cosmology Intertwined III: f(sigma_8) and S_8', Astropart. Phys. 131, 102604 (2021)
